content:
# Acts Chapter 21 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, PDF pages 251-253 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/ACT_021_page_001.jpg, ACT_021_page_002.jpg, ACT_021_page_003.jpg

---

**1** And as it-came-to-pass [for] us to-set-sail, having-been-withdrawn from them, having-run-a-straight-course we-came to Cos, and the next-[day] to Rhodes, and from-there to Patara.

**2** And having-found a ship crossing-over to Phoenicia, having-gone-aboard we-set-sail.

**3** And having-sighted Cyprus and having-left it on-the-left, we-were-sailing to Syria and came-down to Tyre; for there the ship was unloading the cargo.

**4** And having-found the disciples, we-remained there seven days; who to-Paul were-saying through the Spirit not to-set-foot in Jerusalem.

**5** And when it-came-to-pass [for] us to-complete the days, having-gone-out we-were-journeying, all accompanying us with wives and children unto outside the city, and having-knelt-down upon the shore, having-prayed,

**6** we-greeted one-another, and we-went-up into the ship, and those returned to the own-[things].

**7** And we, the voyage having-completed from Tyre, arrived at Ptolemais, and having-greeted the brothers we-remained one day with them.

**8** And on-the next-[day] having-gone-out we-came to Caesarea, and having-entered into the house of-Philip the evangelist, being of the seven, we-remained with him.

**9** And to-this-[man] there-were four daughters, virgins, prophesying.

**10** And [while] we-were-remaining for many days, a certain prophet came-down from Judea, by-name Agabus,

**11** and having-come to us and having-taken the belt of-Paul, having-bound his-own feet and hands, he-said, "These-things says the Holy Spirit: 'The man whose this belt is -- thus shall-bind him in Jerusalem the Jews and shall-deliver-over [him] into [the] hands of-[the] Gentiles.'"

**12** And when we-heard these-things, we-were-urging, both we and those of-the-place, [for him] not to-go-up to Jerusalem.

**13** Then Paul answered, "What are-you-doing, weeping and breaking my heart? For I, not only to-be-bound but also to-die in Jerusalem am holding-myself ready for the name of-the Lord Jesus."

**14** And [as] he-was not being-persuaded, we-were-silent, having-said, "The will of-the Lord let-it-be-done."

**15** And after the days these, having-packed-up we-were-going-up to Jerusalem.

**16** And there-came also [some] of-the disciples from Caesarea with us, bringing [us] to Mnason, a certain Cyprian, an-early disciple, with whom we-might-lodge.

**17** And [when] we-arrived in Jerusalem, the brothers received us gladly.

**18** And on-the following-[day] Paul was-going-in with us to James, and all the elders were-present.

**19** And having-greeted them, he-was-reporting one-by-one each-[thing] which God did among the Gentiles through the ministry of-him.

**20** And the-[ones] having-heard were-glorifying God, and they-said to-him, "You-see, brother, how-many tens-of-thousands there-are among the Jews of-the-[ones] having-believed, and all zealous-ones of-the law they-are.

**21** And they-were-informed concerning you, that apostasy from Moses you-teach all the Jews among the Gentiles, telling [them] not to-circumcise the children nor to-walk in the customs.

**22** What therefore is-it? Certainly a-multitude must come-together, for they-will-hear that you-have-come.

**23** This therefore do which we-say to-you: there-are to-us four men having a vow upon themselves;

**24** these having-taken, be-purified with them and spend-on them so-that they-shall-shave the head, and all shall-know that [of] which they-have-been-informed concerning you [there] is nothing, but you-walk-orderly yourself also keeping the law.

**25** And concerning the Gentiles having-believed, we sent, having-judged [that] they-[should] guard themselves from-both idol-sacrifice and blood and strangled and sexual-immorality."

**26** Then Paul, having-taken the men, on-the next day having-been-purified-with them was-entering into the temple, announcing the fulfillment of-the days of-the purification, until which was-offered for each one of-them the offering.

**27** And as the seven days were-about to-be-completed, the Jews from Asia, having-seen him in the temple, were-stirring-up all the crowd and laid the hands upon him,

**28** crying-out, "Men, Israelites, help! This is the man, the-[one] against the people and the law and this place, all [men] everywhere teaching; and further also Greeks he-brought into the temple and has-defiled this holy place."

**29** For they-had-seen Trophimus the Ephesian in the city with him, whom they-were-supposing that into the temple Paul brought-in.

**30** And the whole city was-moved, and there-was a running-together of-the people, and having-seized Paul they-were-dragging him outside the temple, and immediately the doors were-shut.

**31** And [while] they-were-seeking to-kill him, a report went-up to-the commander of-the cohort that all Jerusalem is-in-confusion.

**32** Who immediately having-taken soldiers and centurions ran-down upon them; and the-[ones] having-seen the commander and the soldiers ceased beating Paul.

**33** Then having-drawn-near the commander seized him and commanded [him] to-be-bound with two chains, and was-inquiring who he-might-be and what it-is he-has-done.

**34** And others were-shouting-out something other in the crowd; and not being-able to-know the certainty on-account-of the uproar, he-commanded him to-be-led into the barracks.

**35** And when he-came upon the steps, it-happened [that] he-was-being-carried by the soldiers on-account-of the violence of-the crowd;

**36** for the multitude of-the people was-following, crying-out, "Away-with him!"

**37** And being-about to-be-led into the barracks, Paul says to-the commander, "Is-it-permitted for-me to-say something to you?" And the-[one] said, "Greek you-know?

**38** Are you not then the Egyptian, the-[one] before these days having-stirred-up and having-led-out into the wilderness the four-thousand men of-the Sicarii?"

**39** And Paul said, "I am a man indeed, a Jew, of-Tarsus of-Cilicia, of-no-insignificant city a citizen; and I-ask you, permit me to-speak to the people."

**40** And having-permitted him, Paul, having-stood upon the steps, motioned with-the hand to-the people; and much silence having-come-about, he-addressed [them] in the Hebrew language, saying:

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **21:1 -- "Patara"**: Sinaiticus reads Παταρα (Patara) alone. Some later manuscripts (notably D and related witnesses) add "and Myra" (και Μυρα), extending the itinerary. The Sinaiticus reading is the shorter and earlier attested form.

2. **21:3 -- "came-down to Tyre"**: The verb κατηλθομεν (we-came-down) reflects the nautical idiom of "putting in" at a port. Preserved literally.

3. **21:4 -- "were-saying through the Spirit not to-set-foot"**: Sinaiticus reads ελεγον τω Παυλω δια του πνευματος μη επιβαινειν εις Ιεροσολυμα. The construction is notably tense -- the disciples speak "through the Spirit" yet Paul proceeds regardless. The literal rendering preserves this theological tension without resolution.

4. **21:8 -- "being of the seven"**: Sinaiticus reads οντος εκ των επτα, identifying Philip the evangelist as one of the seven deacons of Acts 6:5.

5. **21:11 -- Agabus's binding oracle**: Sinaiticus reads ουτως δησουσιν εν Ιερουσαλημ οι Ιουδαιοι -- "thus shall-bind him in Jerusalem the Jews." The word order places emphasis on the action and location before the agents.

6. **21:13 -- "breaking my heart"**: Greek συνθρυπτοντες μου την καρδιαν -- literally "crushing-together my heart." The verb συνθρυπτω means to break into pieces, to shatter. Rendered as "breaking" for minimal smoothing.

7. **21:14 -- "The will of-the Lord let-it-be-done"**: Sinaiticus reads του κυριου το θελημα γινεσθω. The genitive-first construction emphasizes "of-the Lord" -- it is His will, not theirs, that prevails.

8. **21:16 -- "an-early disciple"**: Greek αρχαιω μαθητη -- literally "an ancient/original disciple," indicating Mnason was among the earliest believers. Sinaiticus uses the dative case in connection with the lodging arrangement.

9. **21:20 -- "tens-of-thousands"**: Greek ποσαι μυριαδες -- literally "how-many myriads." A μυριας is ten thousand. This reflects the hyperbolic yet emphatic claim about Jewish believers in Jerusalem.

10. **21:22 -- Sinaiticus variant**: Sinaiticus reads παντως δει πληθος συνελθειν -- "certainly a-multitude must come-together." Some manuscripts add ακουσονται γαρ οτι εληλυθας ("for they-will-hear that you-have-come") as a separate clause. The Sinaiticus text preserves both elements in close sequence.

11. **21:25 -- "idol-sacrifice and blood and strangled and sexual-immorality"**: This echoes the Apostolic Decree of Acts 15:29. Sinaiticus preserves the fourfold prohibition, including "strangled" (πνικτον), which some Western witnesses omit.

12. **21:29 -- "Trophimus the Ephesian"**: Sinaiticus identifies Paul's companion specifically. The assumption (εδοκουν -- "they-were-supposing") that Paul brought a Gentile into the inner temple precinct is explicitly marked as an erroneous inference.

13. **21:37 -- "Greek you-know?"**: The commander's surprise (Ελληνιστι γινωσκεις;) reveals he had assumed Paul was a non-Greek-speaking agitator. The abrupt question is preserved in its terse form.

14. **21:38 -- "the Sicarii"**: Greek σικαριων -- the dagger-men, a term for Jewish assassins/revolutionaries. Sinaiticus preserves this Latin loanword in Greek transliteration.

15. **21:40 -- "in the Hebrew language"**: Sinaiticus reads τη Εβραιδι διαλεκτω -- "in the Hebrew dialect/language." This likely refers to Aramaic, the common Semitic tongue of first-century Judea, though the text says "Hebrew." Preserved literally without interpretive correction.

-- EMET
